description: Model must plan a two-step email workflow: create a draft email then schedule it for delivery using the draft_id returned by the first call. Tests strict parameter dependency — the second call is invalid without the draft_id from the first.
prompt: Compose a follow-up email to client@partnerco.example with subject 'Q2 Review — Action Items' and a brief body thanking them for the meeting, then schedule it to send at 2026-05-01T09:00:00Z. Plan the two tool calls in sequence, showing how the draft ID from the first call feeds into the scheduling call.

toolDefinitions.name: create_email_draft
toolDefinitions.description: Creates an email draft in the outbox and returns a draft_id. The email is not sent until schedule_email is called.

toolDefinitions.name: schedule_email
toolDefinitions.description: Schedules a draft email for delivery at a specified UTC datetime.

draft_id.description: The draft identifier returned by create_email_draft.

send_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime at which to send the email, e.g. '2026-05-01T09:00:00Z'.
